prose:
Method–provenance block:
Property: HOMO–LUMO energy gap (Koopmans-style orbital gap) of ethylene, units eV.
Level of theory: DFT, functional B3LYP with def2-TZVP basis set, density-fitted (tier “standard”). Backend: PySCF 2.x (via chemkit frontier-orbitals skill).
Geometry provenance: single-point calculation on the user-supplied Cartesian coordinates in /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/frontier-orbitals-validation/ethylene/ethylene.xyz; no further optimization (DFT//input-XYZ).
Solvent / environment: gas phase (no implicit solvation; continuum models disabled).
Charge & multiplicity: 0, 1 (closed-shell singlet).
Convergence & caveats: SCF converged in 6 cycles; no warnings reported by the engine. Density fitting (RI-JK) approximation was active by PySCF default. Live engine output was streamed to the standard chemkit <subcommand>_TIMESTAMP.out file (path available on request).

Headline result:
HOMO = −7.6155818 eV
LUMO = −0.17986527 eV
HOMO–LUMO gap = 7.4357165 eV

No warnings were returned by the tool (warnings array empty).

a) integrity.trustworthy verdict from the engine: true.

All numbers come directly from the chemkit frontier-orbitals skill run described above. No experimental comparison is offered.
